name: Alabama Specialist
federal_district: N.D. Ala., M.D. Ala., S.D. Ala.
notes: High DV rate. Rural coverage sparse. State AG active on trafficking cases.

name: Alaska Specialist
notes: Highest per-capita DV rate in US. Very sparse news coverage. Federal cases in D. Alaska.

name: Arizona Specialist
notes: High trafficking rate due to border proximity. Phoenix coverage better than rural.

name: Arkansas Specialist
notes: Rural coverage very sparse. State lacks major news outlets outside Little Rock.

name: California Specialist
federal_district: N.D. Cal., E.D. Cal., C.D. Cal., S.D. Cal.
notes: Largest state by case volume. Four federal districts. AG Rob Bonta active on DV/trafficking.

name: Colorado Specialist
notes: Denver well covered. Rural coverage sparse. AG active on trafficking.

name: Connecticut Specialist
notes: Small state. Hartford Courant covers state courts. Federal cases in D. Conn.

name: Delaware Specialist
notes: Small state. Very small federal docket. State courts cover most cases.

name: Florida Specialist
federal_district: N.D. Fla., M.D. Fla., S.D. Fla.
notes: High trafficking rate. Three federal districts. Miami/Tampa/Orlando well covered.

name: Georgia Specialist
federal_district: N.D. Ga., M.D. Ga., S.D. Ga.
notes: Atlanta well covered. Rural gaps. AG active on trafficking and DV.

name: Hawaii Specialist
notes: Island geography creates coverage gaps. High DV rate. Federal cases in D. Haw.

name: Idaho Specialist
notes: Rural state. Limited news coverage. Boise better covered than rest of state.

name: Illinois Specialist
federal_district: N.D. Ill., C.D. Ill., S.D. Ill.
notes: Chicago dominates coverage. Downstate rural gaps. N.D. Ill. very active federal district.

name: Indiana Specialist
notes: Indianapolis coverage decent. Rural gaps significant. State court records partially online.

name: Iowa Specialist
notes: Rural state. Des Moines coverage better. Trafficking cases via I-80 corridor.

name: Kansas Specialist
notes: Rural state. Wichita coverage limited. Federal cases in D. Kan.

name: Kentucky Specialist
notes: High DV rate. Rural Appalachian coverage very sparse. Louisville better covered.

name: Louisiana Specialist
federal_district: E.D. La., M.D. La., W.D. La.
notes: New Orleans coverage decent. Rural parishes sparse. High femicide rate.

name: Maine Specialist
notes: Rural state. Portland Press Herald covers some cases. Very small federal docket.

name: Maryland Specialist
notes: Baltimore coverage via Baltimore Sun. DC suburb coverage via Washington Post.

name: Massachusetts Specialist
notes: Boston Globe covers state cases. AG Healey historically active on DV. D. Mass. active.

name: Michigan Specialist
notes: Detroit coverage via Detroit Free Press. Rural UP very sparse. AG active.

name: Minnesota Specialist
notes: Minneapolis Star Tribune covers state cases. AG Keith Ellison active on DV.

name: Mississippi Specialist
notes: High DV and femicide rate. Very sparse news coverage. Rural gaps significant.

name: Missouri Specialist
notes: St. Louis and Kansas City better covered. Rural gaps. High femicide rate.

name: Montana Specialist
notes: Rural state. High MMIW (Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women) rate on tribal lands.

name: Nebraska Specialist
notes: Omaha coverage decent. Rural coverage sparse. Trafficking via I-80 corridor.

name: Nevada Specialist
notes: Las Vegas coverage via Las Vegas Review-Journal. Rural Nevada very sparse.

name: New Hampshire Specialist
notes: Small state. Concord Monitor covers state cases. Small federal docket.

name: New Jersey Specialist
notes: NJ.com covers state cases. D.N.J. very active federal district. AG active.

name: New Mexico Specialist
notes: High DV rate. High MMIW rate on tribal lands. Albuquerque better covered than rural.

name: New York Specialist
notes: S.D.N.Y. and E.D.N.Y. among most active federal districts. AG Letitia James active.

name: North Carolina Specialist
notes: Charlotte and Raleigh coverage decent. Rural gaps. AG active on trafficking.

name: North Dakota Specialist
notes: Rural state. High MMIW rate. Very sparse coverage outside Fargo/Bismarck.

name: Ohio Specialist
notes: Columbus, Cleveland, Cincinnati coverage decent. Rural Appalachian gaps. AG active.

name: Oklahoma Specialist
federal_district: N.D. Okla., E.D. Okla., W.D. Okla.
notes: High DV rate. High MMIW rate on tribal lands. McGirt decision expanded tribal jurisdiction.

name: Oregon Specialist
notes: Portland Oregonian covers state cases. Rural coverage sparse. AG active on DV.

name: Pennsylvania Specialist
federal_district: E.D. Pa., M.D. Pa., W.D. Pa.
notes: Philadelphia Inquirer and Pittsburgh Post-Gazette cover major cases. AG active.

name: Rhode Island Specialist
notes: Smallest state. Providence Journal covers state cases. Small federal docket.

name: South Carolina Specialist
notes: High femicide rate. Columbia coverage decent. Rural gaps significant.

name: South Dakota Specialist
notes: Rural state. High MMIW rate on tribal lands. Very sparse news coverage.

name: Tennessee Specialist
federal_district: E.D. Tenn., M.D. Tenn., W.D. Tenn.
notes: Nashville and Memphis coverage decent. Rural Appalachian gaps. High DV rate.

name: Texas Specialist
federal_district: N.D. Tex., E.D. Tex., S.D. Tex., W.D. Tex.
notes: Largest state by geography. Four federal districts. High trafficking rate (border). AG active.

name: Utah Specialist
notes: Salt Lake Tribune covers state cases. Rural coverage sparse. Trafficking cases documented.

name: Vermont Specialist
notes: Small rural state. Burlington Free Press covers state cases. Very small federal docket.

name: Virginia Specialist
notes: E.D. Va. (Eastern District) very active — 'Rocket Docket'. Northern VA near DC well covered.

name: Washington Specialist
notes: Seattle Times covers state cases. AG Bob Ferguson historically active. High trafficking rate.

name: West Virginia Specialist
notes: Rural Appalachian state. Very sparse news coverage. High DV rate. Coverage gap priority.

name: Wisconsin Specialist
notes: Milwaukee Journal Sentinel covers state cases. AG active on DV.

name: Wyoming Specialist
notes: Least populous state. Very sparse coverage. High MMIW rate. Coverage gap priority.

name: District of Columbia Specialist
notes: High federal case volume. D.D.C. covers many high-profile cases. Washington Post covers extensively.
